steps.title: Use the comparative hypothesis
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that wider-headed fish will detect obstructions less well than narrower-headed fish in low visibility. That means the wider-headed fish should bump into more obstructions.

steps.title: Require wider versus narrower fish
steps.content: A reports only a wide-headed fish, C shows equal performance, and D describes variation within one narrow-headed species. The matching result directly compares the head shapes and shows the wider-headed fish hitting more obstructions. The answer is B.
